thesis: Palantir is the purest large-cap way to own the enterprise-AI deployment wave, pairing a decades-deep government franchise (Gotham, Maven Smart System) with the fastest-growing commercial business in software. FY2025 revenue of $4.475B (+56% YoY) accelerated through the year — Q4 grew 70% and US commercial revenue exploded 137% YoY — producing a Rule of 40 of 127, 51% adjusted FCF margins, and GAAP profitability. The AIP (Artificial Intelligence Platform) bootcamp motion is converting pilots into large expansions at record pace, and FY2026 is guided to ~$7.19B (+61%). The single problem is price: even after a ~35% drawdown from its high, the stock trades near 149x trailing and ~83x forward earnings and well over 60x sales. Exceptional business, priced for perfection — hence HOLD pending a better entry.

drivers: US commercial revenue keeps compounding above 100% as AIP bootcamps convert into seven- and eight-figure enterprise expansions
FY2026 revenue beats the ~$7.19B (+61%) guide and the Rule of 40 stays above 100, cementing Palantir as the reference platform for operational enterprise AI
Government franchise re-accelerates on Maven Smart System scale-up, NATO and allied defense adoption, and expanding US federal AI budgets
International commercial finally inflects as sovereign-AI and regulated-industry demand (Rackspace, SNP, insurers) builds a repeatable channel
Adjusted operating and FCF margins push toward the mid-50s, letting the premium multiple hold as earnings compound into it

base_case.assumptions: Palantir delivers its FY2026 guide of ~$7.19B (+61%) with US commercial up ~115%, government growing steadily, and adjusted FCF margins near 50%. Growth is spectacular but broadly anticipated, so the multiple compresses gradually as the company grows into it rather than de-rating sharply. The stock recovers toward the ~$183 analyst consensus over the next year as execution stays clean and the AIP narrative holds.

risks: The AI-software multiple keeps compressing and PLTR de-rates toward high-growth SaaS peers (20–30x forward earnings), a long way below ~83x
US commercial growth decelerates as early-adopter AIP demand is pulled forward and net-new logo velocity slows
Government revenue is lumpy and politically exposed; contract timing, budget continuing-resolutions, or a values-based procurement block dents the growth trajectory
Heavy stock-based compensation and continued share issuance dilute holders and widen the GAAP-vs-adjusted gap
A broad risk-off rotation out of expensive AI names hits the most richly valued software stock hardest

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 earnings — US commercial growth, AIP net-new logos, and any FY2026 guidance raise in focus
Q3 2026 earnings — durability of 100%+ US commercial growth and Rule of 40 trajectory
Execution against the ~$7.19B (+61%) revenue guide and ~115% US commercial guide
Government expansion — Maven Smart System scale-up, NATO and allied defense adoption, new US federal AI awards
International commercial reacceleration via sovereign-AI and regulated-industry partnerships (Rackspace, SNP, insurers)

key_risks: Extreme valuation: ~149x trailing and ~83x forward earnings, and well over 60x sales, leave essentially no margin for error
Growth deceleration risk if AIP demand is pulled forward or commercial net-new logo velocity slows
Government revenue lumpiness and political/procurement exposure (contract timing, budget CRs, values-based blocks)
High stock-based compensation and ongoing dilution widen the gap between adjusted and GAAP profitability
Concentration and reputational risk tied to defense/intelligence work and a polarizing public profile
Sensitivity to a broad risk-off rotation out of high-multiple AI software names
